excerpt: I do not see companions more faithful than mine — Imam al-Ḥusayn at Karbalā
source_work: Rawḍat al-Kāfī and transmitted in Ismaili-Twelver compilations on the virtues of the Imam's supporters

excerpt: Silence is worship without exertion; silence is a fortress without walls — attributed to ʿAlī ibn Abī Ṭālib
Seek not three things, for you shall never find them: a scholar whose knowledge is complete in deed — attributed to ʿAlī
source_work: Nahj al-Balagha and al-Muʾayyad's al-Majālis al-Muʾayyadiyya
source_location_hint: Sermons on the perfection of character

excerpt: Ask me before you are deprived of me — ʿAlī upon the Prophet's pulpit
source_work: Nahj al-Balagha (compiled by al-Sharīf al-Raḍī) and al-Muʾayyad's Majālis

excerpt: Patience is of two kinds: obligatory patience is abandonment of the forbidden; supererogatory patience is abstinence from the permissible — the Prophet (s.a.w.a.)
source_work: Transmitted in Qadi al-Nuʿman's Daʿāʾim al-Islām and al-Muʾayyad's al-Majālis al-Muʾayyadiyya
source_location_hint: Prophetic hadith on degrees of virtue
